attractions.name: Arsenal Hill Park (and Arsenal Hill Community Center)
attractions.description: Arsenal Hill Park is a historic neighborhood park and facility located at the highest point of downtown Columbia. Named for the 1842 military academy once situated there, the park features a bowl-shaped landscape with ample shade from pine trees, a community center, and diverse recreational amenities for all ages. Proudly serving "A Taste of the Southern Low Country," the restaurant specializes in innovative dishes that blend African, West Indian, and Caribbean flavors with Southern traditions. Situated in the historic Seaboard Air Line Railroad Passenger Depot, it offers a classy yet inviting ambiance for both casual dining and special occasions. Blue Marlin, Giftly, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Brookgreen Gardens (Brookgreen Gardens)
attractions.category: Sculpture Garden, Wildlife Preserve, and National Historic Landmark (Brookgreen Gardens About)
attractions.description: Brookgreen Gardens is a unique melding of art, nature, and history, established in 1931 by Archer and Anna Hyatt Huntington. It serves as a premier cultural center featuring the world's largest collection of American figurative sculpture, a Lowcountry zoo, and extensive botanical gardens (Brookgreen Gardens About).
attractions.address: 1931 Brookgreen Drive, Murrells Inlet, SC 29576, USA (Brookgreen Gardens)
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Wednesdays & Saturdays: 9:30 AM - 8:00 PM. Gates close 30 minutes prior to closing time (Brookgreen Gardens).
attractions.admission: Children 3 and under: Free; Children 4-12: $14; Adults 13-64: $25; Seniors 65 & Over: $23. Tickets are valid for 7 consecutive days (Brookgreen Gardens Tickets).
highlights: 'The Huntington Sculpture Garden featuring the largest collection of American figurative sculpture in an outdoor setting ([Wikipedia)', 'The Lowcountry Zoo, home to native species like alligators, bald eagles, and river otters (Brookgreen Gardens)', 'The Creek Excursion, a boat tour through historic rice fields (Brookgreen Gardens Lowcountry History)', 'Botanical Gardens including the Live Oak Allee with trees dating back to the 1700s (Brookgreen Gardens)', 'Lowcountry History and Wildlife Preserve, covering thousands of acres of former plantation land (Brookgreen Gardens Lowcountry History)', 'The Labyrinth, built as an oasis for members and guests (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Wear closed-toe shoes and stay on marked paths for safety around wildlife. Admission tickets are valid for seven consecutive days, making it ideal for multiple visits. No pets are allowed on the property (Brookgreen Gardens Visit).

The restaurant is renowned for its grand atmosphere with soaring ceilings and its signature salad served with warm honey-butter croissants.

attractions.name: Charleston Historic District
attractions.description: The Charleston Historic District is a National Historic Landmark District that serves as the preserved peninsular heart of the city. It features an unparalleled collection of 18th and 19th-century architecture, including its famous "single houses" and cobblestone streets. Visitors can explore a living museum of American history, from its colonial origins to its pivotal roles in the Revolutionary and Civil Wars. Wikipedia, Old Town Trolley Tours
attractions.hours: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM daily Tripadvisor, City of Charleston
attractions.admission: Free (to enter the district; individual museums and house tours have separate fees) Explore Charleston, City of Charleston
highlights: ['Rainbow Row (13 iconic pastel-colored historic homes)', 'The Battery and White Point Garden (scenic waterfront promenade)', 'Charleston City Market (historic public market since the 1790s)', 'The French Quarter (home to art galleries and the Dock Street Theatre)', "St. Michael's Church (the city's oldest surviving church building)", 'Waterfront Park and the Pineapple Fountain']
attractions.tips: The district is best explored on foot to appreciate the detailed architecture and hidden gardens. Use the free CARTA DASH trolley to navigate between key sites without parking hassles, and consider a horse-drawn carriage tour for a narrated historical perspective. Old Town Trolley Tours, Walks of Charleston

attractions.name: Congaree National Park (National Park Service)
attractions.description: Congaree National Park preserves the largest intact expanse of old-growth bottomland hardwood forest remaining in the southeastern United States. This astonishingly biodiverse ecosystem is nourished by the Congaree and Wateree Rivers, which periodically flood the landscape and support the growth of some of the tallest deciduous trees in the country (National Park Service, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 100 National Park Road, Hopkins, SC 29061, United States (National Park Service)
attractions.hours: Park: Open 24 hours daily; Harry Hampton Visitor Center: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily (Closed on New Year's Day, President's Day, and Christmas Day) (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free (No entrance fee is required to access the park) (National Park Service)
highlights: 'Boardwalk Loop Trail: A 2.6-mile elevated walkway through the old-growth forest ([National Park Service)', 'Canoeing and Kayaking: Paddling along Cedar Creek to see towering trees and diverse wildlife (National Park Service)', 'Champion Trees: Home to some of the tallest trees in the eastern U.S., including giant loblolly pines (Wikipedia)', 'Synchronous Fireflies: A rare natural light show occurring for about two weeks each spring (National Park Service)', 'Harry Hampton Visitor Center: Features exhibits on the park’s natural history and a 20-minute informational video (Scho and Jo)', 'Weston Lake: A scenic oxbow lake where otters and wading birds are frequently spotted (National Park Service)']
attractions.tips: Check current water levels and trail conditions before visiting, as much of the park is a floodplain and subject to flooding. Bring insect repellent and consider a bug net during the warmer months when mosquitoes are highly active. If planning to see the synchronous fireflies in late spring, remember to enter the lottery for vehicle passes well in advance (National Park Service, Scho and Jo).

attractions.name: Conquest Brewing Company, Inc.
attractions.description: Established in 2012, Conquest Brewing Company was Columbia's first production craft brewery since Prohibition. The brewery was known for combining science—microbiology, chemistry, and physics—with a passion for creating adventurous and bold beers in a medieval-themed setting near the University of South Carolina stadium.

highlights: ["Columbia's first post-Prohibition production brewery, established in 2012.", 'Flagship beers include the Artemis Blonde, Sacred Heart IPA, and Medusa Stout.', 'Located just blocks from Williams-Brice Stadium, making it a popular pre-game destination.', 'The tasting room featured medieval-themed decor, including cool relics and a casual atmosphere.', 'Offered oversized games like Giant Jenga and Connect 4 for visitors.', 'Hosted a rotating selection of local food trucks for patrons.']
attractions.tips: While the brewery is permanently closed as of 2019, it was best visited on non-game days to avoid extreme crowds. Visitors often recommended trying a flight to sample the diverse range of experimental 'Cyclops' series brews. Always check local listings for neighboring breweries like River Rat and Swamp Cabbage that continue the area's craft beer tradition.

attractions.name: Cottontown/Bellevue Historic District

The neighborhood is protected as an architectural conservation district and is renowned for its well-preserved early 20th-century homes, including Tudor Revival, Colonial Revival, and Craftsman styles (Historic Columbia).

attractions.tips: Plan your visit for the second Saturday in March to experience the Cottontown Art Crawl (Cottontown.us). Grab a local coffee at Indah Coffee or Curiosity Coffee Bar while exploring the district (Yelp).

This tactical "double envelopment" secured a patriot victory on what was then a pasturing area. National Park Service

Opened in 1998, the park features ancient floodplain forest, scenic river overlooks, and a mix of paved and unpaved trails for diverse recreational use. It offers a peaceful, shaded environment for walking, jogging, and exploring the area's rich industrial history. (City of Columbia Parks & Recreation, South Carolina Trails)

attractions.name: Hampton-Preston Mansion and Gardens
attractions.description: The Hampton-Preston Mansion and Gardens is one of Columbia's oldest historic sites, featuring a Federal-era mansion built in 1818 and four acres of meticulously recreated antebellum gardens. Visitors can explore the lives of the elite families who lived there and the enslaved workers who maintained the estate through guided tours and interactive exhibits. The property serves as a hub for horticultural research and historic preservation, showcasing both native and exotic plant collections.

attractions.name: Historic Elmwood Park

Established in the early 20th century, it is renowned for its beautifully restored homes, mature tree-lined streets, and a welcoming 'front porch' culture that encourages a relaxed, sociable way of life. The neighborhood was the first suburb annexed into the city and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.

attractions.name: Kings Mountain National Military Park
attractions.description: Kings Mountain National Military Park commemorates a pivotal victory by American Patriots over American Loyalists during the Southern Campaign of the Revolutionary War. Fought on October 7, 1780, the battle was described by Thomas Jefferson as 'the turn of the tide of success' and effectively ended Loyalist ascendance in the Carolinas (NPS, Wikipedia).

attractions.days_open: Visitor Center: Wednesday - Sunday; Grounds and Trails: Daily (NPS)

attractions.tips: Watch the introductory film at the Visitor Center before walking the trail to provide context for the markers. Bring water as there are no vending machines on-site, and note that while the trail is paved, it contains some steep inclines (Tripadvisor).

Today, it serves as a house museum and outdoor museum that traces the journey of Columbia’s Black community from enslavement through the Civil Rights movement and urban renewal (Historic Columbia).

The estate encompasses 110 acres of formal gardens, a House Museum, and working Stableyards that together interpret the complex history of the Middleton family and the generations of enslaved people who lived and worked there.

attractions.name: Modjeska Monteith Simkins House
attractions.description: The Modjeska Monteith Simkins House is a historic one-story cottage that served as the residence and office for South Carolina's "Matriarch of Civil Rights" from 1932 until 1992. The site was a vital hub for social justice activism, hosting prominent leaders such as Thurgood Marshall and serving as a meeting place for strategizing landmark desegregation cases. Today, it features the multimedia exhibit "An Advocate of the People," which explores Simkins' influential career in public health and human rights advocacy (Historic Columbia, South Carolina ETV).

attractions.name: The Congaree Vista (The Vista)
attractions.description: The Congaree Vista is a vibrant, 1,200-acre historic district in downtown Columbia, South Carolina, known for its energetic and eclectic atmosphere. Once a bustling warehouse district filled with textile mills and railroad stations, it has been transformed into a premier destination for arts, dining, shopping, and entertainment, featuring over 45 restaurants and 60 art galleries and specialty shops.
attractions.hours: District is open 24/7; individual business hours typically range from 10:00 AM to 2:00 AM.
attractions.admission: Free (District access; individual attraction prices vary)
highlights: ['Vibrant arts scene with over 60 galleries and studios including Vista Studios/Gallery 80808.', 'Historic Seaboard Railroad Station housing popular restaurants like Blue Marlin.', "Home to the South Carolina State Museum and EdVenture Children's Museum.", 'Extensive dining options with over 80 restaurants and bars ranging from casual to fine dining.', 'Proximity to the Congaree River and Columbia Canal and Riverfront Park.', 'Annual events such as Artista Vista and the Vista Lights holiday celebration.']
attractions.tips: The district is highly walkable, so park once and explore the 48-block area on foot. Be sure to check out the public art sculptures along Lady Street and look for the 'ChargeBots' at Lewis + Clark studio. Check the local event calendar for live music and gallery openings.

attractions.name: The Museum of the Reconstruction Era at the Woodrow Wilson Family Home
attractions.description: The Museum of the Reconstruction Era is the only museum in the United States dedicated to showcasing the post-Civil War Reconstruction period. Housed in the 1871 Italian villa-style childhood home of President Woodrow Wilson, the site uses the Wilson family's story to explore the profound social, political, and racial changes in Columbia during the late 19th century. Through award-winning exhibits and interactive technology, it aims to dispel prevalent myths about the era and foster dialogue on the evolving meaning of citizenship (Historic Columbia, AASLH).
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed (Gardens: 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.); Tuesday: Closed (Gardens: 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.); Wednesday: 1:30 p.m. Tour (Gardens: 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.); Thursday: 1:30 p.m. Tour (Gardens: 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.); Friday: 1:30 p.m. Tour (Gardens: 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.); Saturday: 1:30 p.m. Tour (Gardens: 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.); Sunday: 1:30 p.m. Tour (Gardens: 1:00 p.m. – 4:00 p.m.) (Historic Columbia)

Residents of Richland and Lexington counties can take advantage of 'Free Sundays' on the third Sunday of each month (Historic Columbia).

attractions.name: Township Auditorium (Columbia Township Auditorium)
attractions.description: The Township Auditorium is a historic multi-purpose performing arts facility that has served as a cultural cornerstone of South Carolina since opening in 1930. Designed by Lafaye & Lafaye, it features a Georgian Revival style and is renowned for its exceptional acoustics and sightlines, hosting everything from legendary musicians like Elvis Presley and Pink Floyd to modern headliners and local graduations (thetownship.org, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday - Friday 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Closed Saturday and Sunday unless there is a scheduled event (thetownship.org, Tripadvisor).
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event; tickets are primarily sold via Ticketmaster. Standard concert seating capacity is 3,072, increasing to 3,383 with general admission floor seating (thetownship.org, Wikipedia).
highlights: 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 2005 ([Wikipedia).', 'Underwent a major $12 million renovation and modernization in 2009-2010 (Wikipedia).', 'Capacity for over 3,000 guests, offering intimate views and superb acoustics (thetownship.org).', 'Rich performance history featuring icons such as Louis Armstrong, Duke Ellington, and James Brown (Wikipedia).', 'Serves as a premier venue for concerts, comedy tours, wrestling, and community events (thetownship.org).', 'Owned and operated by Richland County as a non-profit cultural resource (richlandcountysc.gov).']
attractions.tips: A Clear Bag Policy is strictly enforced for all events. Parking is available in surrounding private lots for a fee (typically $10-$20), and complimentary golf cart shuttles sometimes operate between nearby lots and the entrance. Purchase tickets directly at the Box Office during business hours to avoid some online service fees (thetownship.org, Tripadvisor).

Erected in 1827, it honors Jonathan Maxcy, the first and longest-serving president of South Carolina College, and was designed by renowned architect Robert Mills (University of South Carolina, Historic Columbia).

attractions.admission: Free (Evendo)

It serves as a popular destination for family reunions, camping, and outdoor recreation within the state's capital area. (South Carolina Parks, Experience Columbia SC)

attractions.days_open: Open daily except for Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day (Boone Hall Plantation FAQ)
attractions.admission: Adults (13+): $30.00; Seniors (65+), Military, AAA: $27.00; Children (6-12): $12.00; Children (5 & under): Free (Boone Hall Plantation)
highlights: 'Avenue of Oaks: A 3/4-mile grand entrance lined with moss-draped live oak trees planted in 1743 ([Boone Hall Plantation).', 'Gullah Culture Presentation: Live performances sharing the history and heritage of the Gullah people through storytelling and song (Boone Hall Plantation).', 'Black History in America Exhibit: Self-guided tours through nine original slave cabins exploring the history of enslaved people (Boone Hall Plantation FAQ).', 'Guided Mansion Tour: Tours of the first floor of the 1936 Colonial Revival plantation home (Boone Hall Plantation).', 'Plantation Tractor Tour: A motorized tour around the working farm and beautiful marsh views of the property (Boone Hall Plantation).', 'Butterfly Pavilion: A seasonal attraction showcasing various butterfly species in a natural habitat (The Pinch).']
attractions.tips: Arrive early to schedule your guided house tour at the hospitality center. You can carry your own water but it is not permitted inside the plantation house. Plan to spend several hours to fully explore the 738-acre grounds and attend the live presentations (Reclaiming Wonders, Boone Hall Plantation FAQ).
attractions.nearby: Patriots Point Naval & Maritime Museum, Shem Creek, Arthur Ravenel Jr. Bridge, USS Yorktown, Mount Pleasant Memorial Waterfront Park (Experience Mount Pleasant, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Fort Sumter and Fort Moultrie National Historical Park (formerly Fort Sumter National Monument)
attractions.description: Fort Sumter is a sea fort located in Charleston Harbor, best known as the site where the first shots of the American Civil War were fired on April 12, 1861. Visitors can explore the well-preserved remains of the fort, view historical museum exhibits, and participate in ranger-led programs that detail the fort's strategic importance and its role in redefining American freedom. The site offers a powerful reflection on the divisions that led to the war and the eventual reunification of the nation (NPS, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 am - 4:30 pm, Tuesday: 9:00 am - 4:30 pm, Wednesday: 9:00 am - 4:30 pm, Thursday: 9:00 am - 4:30 pm, Friday: 9:00 am - 4:30 pm, Saturday: 9:00 am - 4:30 pm, Sunday: 9:00 am - 4:30 pm (NPS)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed New Year's Day, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day) (NPS)
attractions.admission: NPS Entrance: Free; Ferry Tickets (Required for access): Adults $43.00, Senior/Active Military $39.00, Children (4-11) $26.00, Children (3 & under) Free (NPS, Fort Sumter Tours)
highlights: ['Site of the first shots of the American Civil War on April 12, 1861', 'Scenic 30-minute ferry ride across Charleston Harbor with skyline views', 'On-site museum featuring artifacts like the original 33-star storm flag', 'Ranger-led interpretive talks and historical presentations at the fort', 'Immersive ruins including original artillery mounts and damaged brick walls', 'Panoramic views of the harbor, Ravenel Bridge, and surrounding forts']
attractions.tips: Advance reservations for the ferry are highly recommended as tours frequently sell out. Arrive at the departure point (Liberty Square or Patriots Point) at least 30 minutes before your scheduled departure. The fort is a trash-free facility with no receptacles, so visitors must carry out any waste. Food is permitted on the ferry but not on the fort grounds (NPS, Fort Sumter Tours).
attractions.nearby: South Carolina Aquarium, Patriots Point Naval & Maritime Museum, Fort Moultrie, Charles Pinckney National Historic Site, Waterfront Park (NPS, TripAdvisor)

attractions.name: Swamp Rabbit Brewery and Taproom
attractions.description: The Swamp Rabbit Brewery and Taproom is a family-owned microbrewery located in the heart of Travelers Rest, South Carolina. Known for its award-winning handcrafted beers and vibrant neighborhood atmosphere, it serves as a popular social hub for locals and cyclists visiting the nearby Swamp Rabbit Trail. (Official Website)
attractions.address: 26 S. Main Street, Travelers Rest, SC 29690
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM; Wednesday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM; Thursday: 12:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Friday: 12:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Saturday: 12:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 7:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: "Award-winning craft beers including the 2018 World Beer Cup Silver Medalist 'Feeling My Oats' ([Official Website)", 'Family and dog-friendly taproom with outdoor seating and a back deck (Tripadvisor)', 'Regular community events such as trivia, bingo, and live music (Official Website)', 'Rotating schedule of local food trucks parked on-site (Tripadvisor)', 'Convenient access for cyclists from the Prisma Health Swamp Rabbit Trail (Visit Greenville SC)', 'Weekly beverage specials including Pitcher Nights and Mimosa deals (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Visit on Thursdays for Trivia or Wednesdays for Pitcher Night specials. The brewery has a private parking lot behind the building, but if full, additional free public parking is available at nearby city lots. While they don't serve their own food, check their schedule for on-site food trucks or bring your own from nearby restaurants. (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Prisma Health Swamp Rabbit Trail, Tandem Creperie and Coffeehouse, The Tasting Room, Gateway Park, Paris Mountain State Park (Tripadvisor)
